title: /set project name: Mobile Detailing App - Project Intake
summary: Next.js app for a mobile detailing business with quote/contact flow, image gallery, testimonials, iPhone calendar sync, and possible customer dashboard.
full_context:
Initial Prompt:
khgg
Short Description:
Next.js app for a mobile detailing business with quote/contact flow, image gallery, testimonials, iPhone calendar sync, and possible customer dashboard.
Users / Audience:
Mobile detailing customers, repeat clients, and large commercial clients.
Core Goal:
Launch a polished single-page mobile detailing site that captures leads, supports quote/contact requests, and lays the groundwork for scheduling and repeat-client account features.
Required Features:
iPhone calender sync, dark theme with light blue accents, qoute/contact form, possible customer dashboard for repeat/large clients. Single page, hero, image modal, testimonies. Hosted on vercel with Mongodb
Nice-to-Have Features:
email server built in, sanitized inputs from forms and submittals to db, place holder modal and testimonies
Preferred Stack:
next.js
Hosting / Infra:
vercel
Design Direction:
dark theme with blue highlights
Known Constraints:
All file writes must occur in "E:\Mobiledets". Hosted on Vercel. MongoDB backend. Single-page v1.
Definition of v1 Done:
A deployable single-page Next.js app on Vercel with hero section, gallery modal, testimonials, quote/contact form, dark theme with blue accents, MongoDB-backed submissions, and a documented plan for calendar sync and dashboard if not included in v1.
proposed_action:
Produce a pr

oject plan for /set project name: Mobile Detailing App.
Return product scope, architecture, phased roadmap, open questions, and a bounded implementation backlog sized for codex-mini.
constraints: Keep outputs concise, direct, and machine-usable.
Do not bypass Notion review or the local mirror.

Plan first. Do not implement until the project plan is approved.
Break approved implementation work into bounded tasks small enough for codex-mini to complete safely.
Do not invent secrets, credentials, legal text, or production-only values.
Escalate if missing information changes architecture, integrations, auth, real assets, or production readiness.
All file writes must occur in "E:\Mobiledets". Hosted on Vercel. MongoDB backend. Single-page v1.
Allowed assumptions:
Placeholder images and testimonials are allowed. Placeholder API keys may be used only in env example files until real credentials are provided.
Escalate to operator if missing:
- Calendar integration method
- whether the dashboard is required in v1
- real API keys
- real branding assets
- and whether placeholders are acceptable in the first implementation.

Created from Telegram project intake.
